title: Dear Diary
label: Full-Stack Web App
summary: A journaling app that reads the mood behind your words with an LLM.

sections.label: Problem
sections.body: Most journaling apps capture words but never the emotional arc behind them. I wanted a journal that quietly surfaces how you've actually been feeling over time — without making you tag or rate anything.

sections.label: Approach
sections.body: A Node.js REST API with JWT auth backs a React front-end. Every entry is sent to the Gemini API for emotion classification, and the detected emotion is rendered as an emoji on a calendar — turning a pile of text into a visual mood timeline.

sections.label: Architecture
sections.body: Entries persist in PostgreSQL on CockroachDB. Passwords are one-way hashed with bcrypt before storage — proper hashing, not reversible encryption — so credentials are unreadable even at the database level. On write, Gemini classifies the entry and the result drives the calendar UI.

sections.label: Challenges
sections.body: Getting the auth and password-security model right (and being precise about hashing vs. encryption), designing a schema that pairs each entry with an emotion, and structuring the LLM call so classifications stay consistent.

sections.label: What's next
sections.body: Richer mood analytics and trend lines, plus an option for on-device or self-hosted classification so entries can stay fully private.

title: Deepfake Detection
label: ML · Full-Stack
summary: A fine-tuned ResNet18 that flags AI-generated images with a confidence score.

sections.label: Problem
sections.body: AI-generated imagery is getting hard to spot by eye. I wanted a tool that returns a defensible confidence score and risk classification — not just a binary yes/no.

sections.label: Approach
sections.body: I fine-tuned a ResNet18 on a labelled dataset of real and AI-generated images, then served it through a FastAPI inference endpoint with a React front-end for upload and results.

sections.label: Architecture
sections.body: The full training pipeline lives in PyTorch — data loading, augmentation, the training loop, and evaluation. The trained model sits behind a FastAPI endpoint that accepts an upload and returns a confidence score plus a risk classification (likely real / likely fake).

sections.label: Challenges
sections.body: Deciding to fine-tune rather than train from scratch (and why ResNet18), shaping an API response that carries both confidence and classification, and keeping upload-to-inference latency reasonable.

sections.label: What's next
sections.body: A larger, more diverse training set, calibration of the confidence scores, and saliency maps to explain why a given image was flagged.

title: Blinking Worlds
label: Game Dev · Computer Vision
summary: A 2D platformer you control by blinking — 1st place at HackBattle.

sections.label: What it is
sections.body: A 2D platformer controlled entirely by real-time eye tracking and hand gestures — no keyboard or controller.

sections.label: The mechanic
sections.body: Blinking (detected via MediaPipe facial landmarks) instantly switches you between two parallel worlds, teleporting you to the same spot in the alternate world. Hand tracking drives movement and actions.

sections.label: Level design
sections.body: Levels intentionally require both worlds — some obstacles can only be cleared by deliberately blinking across at exactly the right moment, turning a technical constraint into the core puzzle.

sections.label: Outcome
sections.body: Built and demoed a fully playable version within the hackathon window, and won 1st place at HackBattle — VIT's largest hackathon — against 200+ participants.

title: V2V Drowsy Driver Alert
label: Embedded · Computer Vision
summary: Detects drowsiness and escalates alerts car-to-car via CSMA/CD.

sections.label: What it is
sections.body: A vehicle-to-vehicle safety system that detects driver drowsiness and triggers an escalating alert chain reaching nearby cars and emergency services.

sections.label: Detection
sections.body: A dashcam feed is processed in real time with OpenCV and MediaPipe to track facial landmarks and detect prolonged eye closure.

sections.label: Escalation
sections.body: A 3-stage chain: 0s local buzzer; 4s broadcast to nearby vehicles over a 2.4GHz channel (their buzzers fire); 10s GPS-tagged distress signal to authorities via a Telegram bot.

sections.label: Networking
sections.body: The inter-vehicle broadcast uses the CSMA/CD protocol to handle collisions and interference when multiple cars transmit on the same channel simultaneously.

title: Smart Tender System
label: Web3 · Blockchain
summary: On-chain government tenders with sealed bids and escrow (prototype).

sections.label: What it is
sections.body: A blockchain-based government tender system designed to replace an opaque process with transparent, tamper-proof smart contracts.

sections.label: Planned flow
sections.body: Vendors log in with MetaMask and are approved on-chain; the government posts tenders; sealed bids stay hidden until the deadline; the winner is chosen by a weighted average of competence score and bid; documents are hashed via IPFS; milestone payments release from escrow.

sections.label: Built so far
sections.body: Smart contracts, vendor MetaMask login, and Pinata document hashing. Bidding, weighted scoring, milestone tracking, and escrow were designed but not fully implemented.

sections.label: Honest framing
sections.body: This is a prototype — I'm explicit about what's actually built versus what was designed on paper.

title: Speech Therapy Backend
label: AI · Mobile Backend
summary: Hindi speech → STT → Gemini-generated personalised therapy exercises.

sections.label: What it is
sections.body: The backend for a Hindi-language speech therapy app that evaluates a user's speech and dynamically generates personalised therapy exercises.

sections.label: The pipeline
sections.body: A React Native app captures Hindi voice input; Google STT transcribes it with a confidence score; that score and transcription feed the Gemini API, which generates the next personalised exercise. The full voice → STT → scoring → Gemini → exercise pipeline is built and tested.

sections.label: Status
sections.body: Frontend UI and a data-persistence layer (storing user progress) remain — framed honestly as ongoing work.
